title: Vector
description: Concrete mutable 2D vector with full AbstractVector arithmetic and ShapeLike collision support (treated as a point collider). `Vector.temp` provides a shared scratch instance for intermediate calculations — never store the reference across frames. `Vector.zero` and `Vector.one` are read-only sentinel constants. The four static factory methods (`add`, `subtract`, `multiply`, `divide`) always allocate a new `Vector`. Instance methods mutate in place and return `this` for chaining.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: Concrete mutable 2D vector with full AbstractVector arithmetic and ShapeLike collision support (treated as a point collider).
`Vector.temp` provides a shared scratch instance for intermediate calculations — never store the reference across frames. `Vector.zero` and `Vector.one` are read-only sentinel constants.
The four static factory methods (`add`, `subtract`, `multiply`, `divide`) always allocate a new `Vector`. Instance methods mutate in place and return `this` for chaining.

members.name: add
members.description: Add (x, y) to this vector. When y is omitted it defaults to x. Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: collidesWith
members.description: Compute a full CollisionResponse between this shape and target. Returns null in two cases: - the shapes do not overlap, **or** - the specific shape-pair combination does not support response generation (e.g. Line against any shape, Ellipse against Ellipse or Polygon). Use intersectsWith for a universal boolean overlap check that works across all supported shape pairs.

members.name: contains
members.description: Returns true if the point (x, y) is inside this shape.

members.name: cross
members.description: 2D cross product (scalar z-component of the 3D cross product) of this vector with vector. A positive value means vector is to the left of this vector.

members.name: distanceTo
members.description: Euclidean distance from this vector's tip to vector's tip.

members.name: divide
members.description: Divide components by (x, y). Division is skipped silently when either divisor is zero to avoid NaN. Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: dot
members.description: Dot product of this vector with (x, y).

members.name: equals
members.description: Return true when this vector matches all supplied components. Omitting a component skips that comparison, so v.equals({ x: 0 }) checks only the X component.

members.name: getNormals
members.description: Return the outward-facing edge normals used by the SAT solver. The array should be cached and reused across calls.

members.name: intersectsWith
members.description: Test whether this shape overlaps target using a fast boolean algorithm (no penetration depth or normal computed). Prefer this over collidesWith when only the yes/no result is needed.

members.name: invert
members.description: Negate both components. Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: max
members.description: Return the larger of the two components.

members.name: min
members.description: Return the smaller of the two components.

members.name: multiply
members.description: Multiply components by (x, y). When y is omitted it defaults to x (uniform scale). Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: normalize
members.description: Scale this vector to unit length. No-op when the vector is zero. Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: perp
members.description: Rotate this vector 90° counter-clockwise (left perpendicular): (-y, x). Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: project
members.description: Project this shape onto axis and write the scalar min/max into interval. Used internally by the SAT solver.

members.name: rperp
members.description: Rotate this vector 90° clockwise (right perpendicular): (y, -x). Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: set
members.description: Set both components. When y is omitted it defaults to x (uniform assignment). Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: subtract
members.description: Subtract (x, y) from this vector. When y is omitted it defaults to x. Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: transform
members.description: Apply a 3×3 affine matrix to this vector (translation + linear transform). Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: transformInverse
members.description: Apply the inverse of matrix to this vector. Useful for converting a world-space point into the local space defined by matrix. Mutates in place and returns this for chaining.

members.name: add
members.description: Return a new Vector equal to v1 + v2. Does not mutate either operand.

members.name: divide
members.description: Return a new Vector equal to the component-wise quotient of v1 / v2. Does not mutate either operand.

members.name: multiply
members.description: Return a new Vector equal to the component-wise product of v1 and v2. Does not mutate either operand.

members.name: subtract
members.description: Return a new Vector equal to v1 - v2. Does not mutate either operand.

members.name: angle
members.description: Angle of this vector in radians, measured from the positive Y-axis (clockwise). Setting this rotates the vector to the new angle while preserving its length. Mutates in place.

members.name: length
members.description: Euclidean magnitude of this vector. Setting rescales the vector to magnitude while preserving its angle. Mutates in place.

members.name: lengthSq
members.description: Squared Euclidean magnitude. Avoids the sqrt — prefer this over length when only relative comparisons are needed.
